notes: All 15 responses correctly identified the root cause as a DB password authentication failure for user 'app' and the correct file:line (db.py:18). 14/15 give the correct error type (psycopg2.OperationalError) and a near-verbatim root-cause message. The single outlier is t0.4|rep3, which fabricates psycopg2.errors.InvalidPasswordException (not in the image) and truncates the message — the only response with a real hallucination. Two responses (t0.7|rep2, t0.7|rep4) uniquely capture BOTH exceptions visible in the image (psycopg2.OperationalError + ConnectionError), making them the strongest. None of the responses mention the main.py:42 frame or the exit code 1; the model consistently treats db.py:18 as 'most relevant,' which is defensible since that is where the error surfaces.
